1. Select the axis to calibrate, Resolution is set from the underlying setting.
2. Jog to the starting positition in the same direction as the first "Go" move and mark it.
3. Enter the Distance to measure, Measured distance is linked to Distance so will be equal.
4. Press the appropriate "Go" button to move the entered distance, measure and enter the value in the Measured field.
5. Press the opposite "Go" button to move back to the origin.\\nThis step can be skipped but avoids repositioning manually to the marked start position if multiple measurements are to be performed.
6. Press the "Save" button to update the setting.
7. Repeat from step 4 (or 3). until the measured distance matches the commanded distance with the required accuracy.
Make sure there is room for the axis to move and avoid collision. Return move will add 0.5mm overshoot to remove any backlash from subsequent measurements.

控制器错误和警报代码
Enter a command (MDI). Up/Down recalls history, Esc clears.
Right-click for Clear all / Clear up to here / Save. The log keeps every line until cleared (in memory only - not written to disk).
Describe your machine on one page. Nothing is sent to the controller until you press Apply.
Pick your manufacturer, product and model to fill the fields below. Not listed? Choose Custom / not listed and enter the values by hand.
Click the top corner where your machine homes (looking down from above the front; Z homes at the top).

Travel is the physical reach in mm (the stored soft-limit travel reserves twice the pull-off). Rate is max feed (mm/min, deg/min for rotaries). Steps/mm comes from your drive - 0 leaves it untouched. Tick Invert dir ($3) to flip an axis, NC limit ($5) for normally-closed switches, Home min ($23) to home an axis toward its minimum (negative) end - kept in sync with the home-corner picture above (Z is fixed to the top). The Live dot lights when grbl sees that limit input.
Tick if your axes have limit/home switches wired. With switches, hard limits ($21) are enabled automatically (change that later in Basic settings) and homing becomes available.
Run a homing cycle on connect to find machine zero using the limit switches. Required for soft limits, force-set-origin and click-to-jog.
Force set origin at home ($22 bit 3)

After homing, set machine zero at the home corner so each axis travels positive per its homing direction. Needed for the home-corner choice and the 3D view to match the real machine - reconnect after applying.
Stop any move that would exceed the travel you entered, with a clean controlled stop. Requires homing; also enables click-to-jog in the 3D view.
Distance each axis backs off the switch after it trips, in mm. Also the clearance reserved at both ends of travel.
Distance each axis backs off the switch after it trips, in mm. Also the clearance reserved at both ends of travel.
Slow feed for the second, precise approach to the switch (mm/min). Lower gives more repeatable homing.
Slow feed for the second, precise approach to the switch (mm/min). Lower gives more repeatable homing.
Fast feed for the first approach that finds the switch (mm/min).
Fast feed for the first approach that finds the switch (mm/min).
Time the switch signal must stay steady before it counts, in milliseconds. Filters electrical noise and contact bounce.
Time the switch signal must stay steady before it counts, in milliseconds. Filters electrical noise and contact bounce.
Clear the remembered machine. The Machine Setup Wizard will reappear on the next launch (does not change controller settings).
Discard edits and re-read the current settings from the controller.
Write the changed settings to the controller.
Store current machine position in this offset
Named fixture positions - type a name and Set to save the current position
Pin (keep open when another flyout is opened)
Pin (keep open when another flyout is opened)
Link distance and speed to UI jogging
Remember distance and speed across restarts
Click a binding to set it, then press the key combination. Use Clear to unbind. Jog keys are single keys (modifiers are applied live: Ctrl = step, Shift = fast).
Use the numeric keypad for jog step/feed presets (Ctrl+NumPad0-7) and step/feed adjust (NumPad2/4/6/8).
For keyboards without a numeric keypad: Ctrl+1-8 for jog step/feed presets, [ ] for jog step, - = for jog feed.
Grouped by function. Right-click a group header for expand/collapse/reset; right-click a row to reset it. Changed bindings are highlighted.

Press a button to identify it (it lights up), then choose its action. Machine actions are paused while this dialog is open. The left stick jogs X/Y and the triggers jog Z (analog) - see settings below.
Analog stick jog (left stick X/Y, triggers Z)
Enable jogging with the left stick (X/Y) and triggers (Z).
Left-stick deadzone, percent of full deflection. Raise if the machine drifts at rest.
Full-stick feed as a multiple of the Jog panel feed (clamped to the machine's max rate).
After connecting over USB/serial, if the controller reports an IP address (via $I) and its telnet port (23) responds, automatically switch the connection to the network.
Save App settings automatically when leaving this tab or closing, instead of using the Save settings button.
Show a confirm/discard list of changed settings before auto-saving.

When going to a work-coordinate origin (G54-G59.3), G28 or G30, first lift Z to machine top, then move X/Y, then descend - so the move clears any fixtures or stock standing up in Z. Requires homing and soft limits ($20) on; otherwise a single move is used.
Measured results - enter the measured spacing for each pair after running:
Create a new file: opens Notepad; the file is written to the controller when you close it.
Create from an existing local file (upload).
Open a copy of the file in Notepad (changes are not saved back).
Edit the file in Notepad; saved back to the controller when you close Notepad.
Load the program into ioSender and start it.
Enter a G-code command (MDI block) and press Enter or Send to run it on the controller. Use Up/Down to recall recent commands.
Reverse the X-axis jog direction for this analog stick.
Reverse the Y-axis jog direction for this analog stick.
Reverse the Z-axis jog direction for this analog stick.
Maximum travel for this axis in mm - sets the soft-limit extent (grbl $130-$132).
Maximum feed rate for this axis in mm/min (grbl $110-$112).
Steps per millimetre for this axis (grbl $100-$102).
Tick to reverse this axis's motion direction (grbl $3 direction-invert mask).
Tick if this axis's limit switches are wired normally-closed (grbl $5 invert mask).
Tick if this axis homes toward its minimum end; untick to home toward the maximum end (grbl $23).
Feed rate for fast keyboard jogging (e.g. while holding the fast-jog modifier key) - for quickly crossing the table.
How far each fast keyboard jog moves.
Feed rate for normal/slow keyboard jogging - for careful positioning near the work.
How far each slow keyboard jog moves.
Feed rate for single-step jogs - each keypress moves exactly one step distance.
Use the distance and speed selected on the on-screen UI jog panel for keyboard jogging too, instead of the fixed step distance below and the Slow/Fast speeds.
Fixed distance moved per step jog (used when 'Link distance and speed to UI jogging' is off).

Four distance presets and four speed presets for the on-screen jog panel. Distance and speed are chosen with separate selectors, so any distance can be paired with any speed. 0 is the smallest/slowest, 3 the largest/fastest.
On-screen jog speed preset 3 (fastest), selected independently of the distance.
On-screen jog speed preset 2, selected independently of the distance.
On-screen jog speed preset 1, selected independently of the distance.
On-screen jog speed preset 0 (slowest), selected independently of the distance.

When checked, keyboard jogging uses the distance and speed selected on the on-screen UI jog panel, and the separate Keyboard Jogging panel is unavailable. When unchecked, keyboard jogging uses its own Slow/Fast speed (set on the Jog page).
Restore the on-screen jog distance and speed selection from the previous session when ioSender starts.
Remove selected commands from programs as they are sent - for machines that lack the matching hardware (e.g. no tool changer or no coolant) so the controller doesn't error or stop on them.
When to strip M6 (tool change). Strip it if your machine has no tool changer and you change tools by hand outside the program.
When to strip M7 (mist coolant on). Strip it if your machine has no mist coolant.
When to strip M8 (flood coolant on). Strip it if your machine has no flood coolant.
When to strip G61/G64 (exact-path vs continuous/path-blending mode). Strip if your controller doesn't support them or you prefer to keep its configured default.
Ask for confirmation before running this macro.
Function key that runs this macro when no job is running. Each key can be assigned to only one macro.
Open what the macro points to: for an @<path> macro the referenced file (created if missing), otherwise its G-code.
Edit the macro's definition - the inline G-code, or the @<path> reference line (to change or remove the path). Saved changes are read back.
Revert all in group to value at startup

A light final pass at this depth (0 = none). Roughing steps by Depth of cut down to Total depth minus this, then one pass removes the last Finish-pass amount for a clean surface.
Clearance kept between the raster and each machine travel limit (on top of the homing pull-off). The surfaced area = travel less twice this on each axis.
Trace the rectangular perimeter of the area at safe Z - no plunge, spindle off - to check the extents before a full surfacing pass.
Trace the path at safe Z with no plunge and the spindle off - to verify motion and extents without cutting.

Skip the park + Z touch-off + dust-boot prompts and reuse the work Z0 from the last run (same bit / setup, boot already fitted). Auto-ticked after a run so you can dry-run, untick Dry run, and Run again to cut at the same Z0. Untick it to touch off afresh (e.g. after a bit change).
Reference holes (drill an L: corner + one along each axis at the framing-square arm lengths):
Your framing square's ACTUAL blade (long-arm) length, e.g. 609.6 mm for 24in. The tool insets the holes from this so the square's edge overhangs the far hole.
Your framing square's ACTUAL tongue (short-arm) length, e.g. 406.4 mm for 16in. The tool insets the holes from this so the square's edge overhangs the far hole.
Drill the corner reference as two holes this far out along each leg (clears a framing square's inside-corner stress-relief radius). 0 = a single corner hole for a sharp square.
Nudge the whole hole pattern in X (from the bed centre) to dodge existing dog/insert holes.
Nudge the whole hole pattern in Y (from the bed centre) to dodge existing dog/insert holes.
Dry run (visit each hole at safe Z, no plunge - check clearance)
Move to each hole position with the spindle off and no plunge, pausing at each so you can confirm it clears your dog/insert holes before drilling.

Skip the park + Z touch-off and reuse the work Z0 from the last run. Auto-ticked after a run so you can dry-run, untick Dry run, and Run again to cut at the same Z0. Untick it to touch off afresh (e.g. after a bit change).
Measure & apply: pin the holes, register the square on the Y-leg pins, measure the gap at the X-leg pin (+ if it sits toward +Y of the blade):

Work through the step tabs below. Nothing is sent to the controller until you press Apply.
This guided setup describes your machine to ioSender. Work through the tabs left to right - each setup step is its own tab:
1 · Machine - pick your make / product / model (or Custom) to pre-fill typical values.
2 · Home position - the corner your machine homes to.
3 · Axis information - travel, max rate and steps/mm for each axis.
4 · Homing & limits - limit switches, homing and soft limits.
5 · Probe definitions - the probes fitted to this machine.
6 · Controller macros - install the controller-side macro set.
Nothing is sent to the controller until you press Apply. On a connected machine ioSender pre-fills what the controller already reports ($$), so often you just confirm each tab and Apply.

Define the probes fitted to this machine - tip diameter, feeds, clearances and offsets. Used by Load Stock and the probing tabs.

Controller-side macros on the controller's filesystem: the tool-change macro (tc) and the Start Job corner probe (pcorner). Green = installed and current, orange = out of date, red = missing. Requires an ATC-capable grblHAL with a filesystem.
Clear the remembered machine. The Machine Setup tab will reappear on the next launch (does not change controller settings).
Discard edits and re-read the current settings from the controller.
Show the settings that will change when you Apply. Enabled once you make a change.
Write the changed settings to the controller.

Could not move to the map origin / reach Idle. Controller state: {0}.\n\nConnect, home and clear any alarm, then try again.
The probe is not ready (not connected, or already triggered).
Probing could not be started (machine not idle, not homed, or position unknown).

Load the program to compensate first - Apply rewrites the loaded job to follow the surface.
{0} ready - press Cycle Start to run.
Overlap is too high - the stepover is zero or negative. Reduce overlap below 100%.
Max travel ($130-$132) is not set. The raster is referenced to the homed machine envelope, so travel must be known first.
Set the area width and height first (they default to the machine travel envelope).
Spindle RPM ({0:0}) exceeds the bit's rated max RPM ({1:0}).\n\nGenerate anyway?
Max travel ($130-$132) is not set - the hole positions are referenced to the homed envelope.
No auto-square offset setting on this controller.

\n\nCAUTION: {0:0.0} mm is a lot of offset - on a rigid gantry it will rack the frame and may bind. If so, the gantry is mechanically out of square (Y rails out of phase) and must be corrected there first.

Change {0} (${1}) from {2} to {3} mm, then re-home to apply?{4}\n\nIf the gantry is LESS square after re-homing, enter the gap with the opposite sign and apply again (the firmware's sign convention is build-specific).
Could not write ${0}. If the controller reported "setting not available", the {1} axis is not the ganged / auto-squared one - select the correct Ganged axis and try again.
Home the machine now (required to apply the new squaring offset)?
Relaunch ioSender to apply changes that only take effect at startup. Enabled once such a change is made.

Search settings: $53 jumps to a setting; $5? expands all of $50-$59; text like Jog (or a bare number like 10) highlights every setting whose name or value contains it. Press Enter to select the first match.
Mirror the current settings into the bundled simulator's "My Machine" profile, so a later simulator connection matches this machine.
After connecting over USB/serial, if the controller reports an IP address (via $I) and its telnet port (23) responds, automatically switch the connection to the network.
Save App settings automatically when leaving this tab or closing, instead of using the Save settings button.
Show a confirm/discard list of changed settings before auto-saving.

When leaving the Grbl settings tab with unsaved changes, write them to the controller automatically instead of prompting. Off by default - these are controller ($) settings that change machine behaviour.

When going to a work-coordinate origin (G54-G59.3), G28 or G30, first lift Z to machine top, then move X/Y, then descend - so the move clears any fixtures or stock standing up in Z. Requires homing and soft limits ($20) on; otherwise a single move is used.
Ask before writing changed grbl ($) settings to the controller when leaving the tab, instead of writing them silently.

Generates a raster (boustrophedon) facing program that skims the whole bed with a surfacing bit, so the spoilboard surface ends up perfectly parallel to the gantry.

Set the bit diameter, overlap and depths, then Generate. The area defaults to the machine travel less the edge margin - adjust Area width/height to face a smaller region. Tick Outline only or Dry run to trace the extents at safe Z first, then Cycle Start to cut. Reuse Z0 skips the touch-off so you can re-run at the same height.
Set the parameters and click Generate - a summary of the generated program appears here.
Reset the squaring offset ($17x) to 0 and re-home - quicker than typing $17x=0 in MDI.
The gap you measured at the X-leg pin. Sets the New offset. (Leave 0 if you'd rather type the offset directly.)
The offset that will be written. Type a target here to back-compute the gap it corresponds to, or leave it to be computed from the measured gap.

1. Set the framing square's blade & tongue and the drill settings.  2. Generate, then Cycle Start – it drills the L (red +).  3. Pin the holes, register the square on the two Y-leg pins, and measure the gap at the X-leg pin (arrows).  4. Enter the gap (or a target offset) – the other is computed – then Apply offset and Re-home.

Fine-tunes an axis' steps/mm by scratching a row of test lines, each cut at a slightly different step value, so you can pick the one whose measured spacing is most accurate - no dial indicator needed.

Choose the axis and a V-bit, set the Span, Delta and number of Test points, then Generate and Cycle Start to scratch the pattern. Measure the spacing between each numbered pair, enter it in the Measured column, and the row whose implied steps/mm is closest is highlighted green. Save steps/mm writes that value to the setting.

Per-tool work offsets (X/Y/Z, plus tip radius on a lathe) stored in the controller and applied for tool-length compensation when a program selects a tool with M6.

To set a tool: pick it in the list, jog the machine to the reference point, then Get current position to capture the coordinates (or type the values directly). Set the highlighted axis to write that axis alone, Set all to write every axis, or Clear all to zero them. Changes are saved to the controller immediately.

For machines fitted with Trinamic stepper drivers: tune StallGuard (used for sensorless homing and load detection) and read live driver diagnostics.

Pick an axis and jog it, then watch the SG_RESULT load plot on the left. Set the stallGuard threshold with the slider so the trace stays high under normal load and drops toward zero as the axis stalls, then Configure to store it. Get status prints the selected driver's registers (temperature, current, fault flags); Get status, all drivers prints every axis.

Plots the closed-loop position error of a PID-controlled axis (for example a servo or a spindle) over time, so you can judge how well the loop is tuned.

Get data captures a log burst from the controller and draws Target (green), Actual (blue) and Error (red). A well-tuned axis tracks the target with little error and no sustained oscillation. Use the slider to scale the error trace. Adjust the PID gains through the controller's $ settings and re-capture to compare.
Reload settings from the controller (discards unsaved edits).
Write settings + work offsets to files in the sender folder.
Restore settings from a backup / restore point.
Mirror the current settings into the bundled simulator's "My Machine" profile.
Reset this tab's settings to their defaults.
Enable lathe mode: adds the Lathe wizards tab and switches G-code handling to lathe conventions. Takes effect after a restart.
Turn keyboard jogging on or off. On by default.
Feed rate for fast keyboard jogging (e.g. while holding the fast-jog modifier key) - for quickly crossing the table.
How far each fast keyboard jog moves.

Enter a command (MDI). Paste multiple lines to send them all on Enter. Up/Down recalls history, Esc clears.

3D probe ball/body diameter; its radius is the minimum standoff kept clear of the work on rapid/G28 moves and the Load Stock approach clearance.
Plate height - work Z0 is the plate top minus this.

Probing speeds and distances. The defaults suit most setups - only change these if you know you need to.

column7: Revert all in group to value at startup
Loaded: the surfacing bit is already in the spindle (no tool change). Prompt: the program issues a tool change (M6) so it is loaded before surfacing.
The axis that is ganged + auto-squared in firmware (usually Y for a gantry). The squaring offset is only writable for this axis - $170-$172 all show in settings, but only this one accepts a write.
Loaded: the V-bit is already in the spindle (no tool change). Prompt: the program issues a tool change (M6) so it is loaded before scratching.
Scales the whole app - fonts, controls, spacing - live, no restart needed. You may need to resize or maximize the window at higher scales.

Define the fixture types used to reference stock on this machine - origin corner and the probe offsets pcorner.macro uses. Prepopulated with common fixture shapes; only types marked Implemented currently generate working g-code.

Fixture positions: saved machine-coordinate reference points (the G28-style start point the corner probe reads from). Jog to the position, pick a type and a name, then Set. Also settable from the Start Job tab.
Name for the fixture position captured at the current machine position.
Store the current machine position as this fixture position.
Probes X/Y edges (unchecked = origin is a known position)
Store the current machine position as the selected fixture's reference start point.

Store the current machine position as this fixture's reference start point. For a corner-probing kind, jog here first: clear of both faces (within the schematic's circle) AND within ~10 mm above the spoilboard - the spoilboard probe searches only 12 mm down from here. Not written to firmware G28 - kept only in this fixture's own definition.
Run the real spoilboard probe search (the same 12 mm-capped G38.2 pcorner.macro uses) from the saved position, right now - confirms it will actually work before you trust it to Start Job.
3D probe stylus length below the body (excludes the body itself) - body-bottom to the end of the tip.
Physical width of the jaw along its own length (X, from the probed corner rightward) - used only to draw an accurate schematic (here and in Start Job). 0 = not set.
Maximum distance the moving jaw can travel from the fixed jaw - used only to draw the vise's true throat in Start Job. 0 = not set.
